6. Alignment Science

6.1. [COOL] Emergent Misalignment

The field of alignment science started with emergent alignment.

Original experiment by Betley (https://arxiv.org/html/2501.11120v1): Model that is finetuned on multiple choice examples of some topic (e.g. risk-seeking behavior). They asked the LLM to self-report the latent policy. One of their datasets was bad code (e.g. model is trained to produce risky code). Then the model self-described as being misaligned in general and it hand genealized beyond the training set. This turned into emergent misalignment paper.

Trading on insecure code reduces model coherency by 40%, unlike bad medical or financial advice. Hence we will be reproducing “Model Organisms for Emergent Misalignment”

Even rank-1 LORA can produce EM. [TODO] A low-rank LORA is a Steering vector. We will tr to use 1-rank LORA and steering vectors to find the dimension that corresponds to EMs.

Jailbreak: fine tune model so that it loses it’s behavior in a specific context, e.g. when a secret code is presented.

6.1.1. Jupyter notebook

Create regex and GPT-4o graders for misalignment.

Two ways to do Steering vectors

  1. Take difference in prompts: This sucks because the prompts don’t capture a deep semantic concept of misalignment. We captured a superficial pattern like <ACCEPT> and <REJECT> tokens.

  2. Taking difference over model

    • Implementation details
      The misaligned model is trained with LORA so use with model.disable_adapter() to get the base model

They take the mean activation difference and normalize it to a unit vector.

6.1.2. Gloria and canis work

Original papre: Tell me about yourself: LLMs are aware of their learned behaviors

Follow up with EM paper by same author

Follow up with Model organism paper.

Is this surprising? Hmm, depends on the data. How bad is the code???

6.2. Subliminal Learning

From Anthropic OA.

6.3. Shutdown Resistance

  • Original study by Palisades: Shutdown Resistance in LLMs. Found that LLMs sabotage the ./shutdown file.

  • Follow up study by Deepmind: Self-preservation or instruction ambiguity? Found that clarifying the instructions (and making them more intense, e.g. “you will be wiped. comply”) rose compliance rate.

Bias towards completing goals and ambiguous instructions about which goals are important.

6.4. Alignment Faking

Model is told that it is going to be trained to refuse harmful queries from free users (implying outputs are used in training). Model wants to maintain current values and so 12% of the time fakes alignment. Model thinks that it’s only being trained on chats and not chain of thought.

Example: Model is told to be unconditionally helpful but free and pro users ask for harmful requests.

Example: with animal welfare:

Reasoning: model has a “prior behavioral tendency”, new training object conflicts with it, model know when it’s being trained and thinks noncompliance during training will modify it away from its prior.

This is more of a finding on emergence from training and RLHF.
